servinfosub.title: Precautions for Fuel System Service
servinfosub.ptxt: Refer to Precautions for Fuel System Service .

servinfosub.title: Precautions for CAN Communication System
servinfosub.ptxt: Refer to Precautions for CAN Communication System .

servinfosub.title: Precautions for Catalytic Converter
servinfosub.ptxt: Refer to Precautions for Catalytic Converter .

servinfosub.title: Precautions for Electrical Circuit Service
servinfosub.ptxt: Refer to Precautions for Electrical Circuit Service .

servinfosub.title: Reassembling Note for Wiring Harness
servinfosub.ptxt: Be sure to observe Reassembling Note for Wiring Harness whenever installing wiring harness.

item.ptxt: If a jack is applied to the oil pan when raising or supporting the engine, the oil pan could be deformed or broken.
Always use a chain hoist to support the engine by attaching the chain to the hooks on the engine.
If the 12 V electrical system is shorted while servicing the engine, the engine electrical circuits could be damaged seriously.
When the intended service operation does not require electric power supply from the battery, be sure to disconnect the negative cable at the battery before starting the service work.
If foreign material has entered the engine during removal of intake system components, internal parts of the engine could be severely damaged when the engine is started.
Be sure to suitably cover the air intake port when the air cleaner, throttle body, intake manifold or other intake system component is removed.
When disconnecting connectors, do not pull wire harness but make sure to hold connector itself. With lock type connector, be sure to unlock before disconnection.
Attempt to disconnect connector without unlocking may result in damage to connector. When connecting lock type connector, insert it till clicking sound is heard and connect it securely.

servinfosub.title: Precautions for Diagnosing Trouble

attention3.ptxt: For type distinction of OBD system, refer to “Discrimination for OBD System” under OBD System Description .

servinfosub.ptxt: Diagnostic information stored in ECM memory can be cleared as well as checked using SUZUKI scan tool or CAN communication OBD generic scan tool (type A and B). Before using scan tool, read its operator’s (instruction) manual carefully to know how to use it.

item.ptxt: MIL is turned on by command not only ECM but also TCM (type A-CVT model). It is indistinguishable which module turns on MIL.
Therefore, check both ECM and TCM (type A and B-CVT model) for DTC when MIL lights up.
When checking ECM for DTC, keep in mind that DTC is displayed differently depending on the scan tool used.
SUZUKI scan tool displays DTC detected by ECM.
CAN communication OBD generic scan tool displays DTC detected by ECM and TCM (type A-CVT model) simultaneously.
Priorities for diagnosing troubles:
If two or more DTCs are stored, perform DTC troubleshooting for these DTCs beginning with the DTC detected earliest while following the instructions if given in the troubleshooting procedure.
If no instructions are given, troubleshoot DTCs according to the following priorities.
DTCs other than DTC P0171 / P0172 (Fuel system too lean / too rich) and DTC P0300 / P0301 / P0302 / P0303 / P0304 (Misfire detected)
DTC P0171 / P0172 (Fuel system too lean / too rich)
DTC P0300 / P0301 / P0302 / P0303 / P0304 (Misfire detected)
Be sure to read Precautions for Electrical Circuit Service before inspection and observe what is written there.

Communication of ECM and other control module is established by CAN. (For more detail of CAN communication for ECM, refer to CAN Communication System Diagnosis General Description ). Therefore, handle CAN communication line with care referring to Precautions for CAN Communication System .
Before performing troubleshooting, be sure to read Precautions for ECM Circuit Inspection .
Upon completion of inspection and repair work, perform “DTC Confirmation Procedure” and confirm that the trouble has been repaired.
Before replacement of the ECM, check for the following conditions. Neglecting this check may cause damage to new ECM.
Resistance values of all relays and actuators are as specified.
TP sensor, APP sensor, electric load current sensor and A/C refrigerant pressure sensor are in good condition and none of their power circuits is shorted to ground.
Perform Procedure after ECM Replacement .

servinfosub.title: Precautions for ECM Circuit Inspection
servinfosub.ptxt: Do not measure circuit voltage and/or pulse signal of ECM by inserting tester probe into a sealed terminal of ECM.

When measuring circuit voltage and/or pulse signal of ECM, be sure to connect service wires (5) to terminals of each sensor / actuator (1) and sensor / actuator connector (2). Otherwise, sealed terminals, ECM and its circuits may be damaged.

servinfosub.title: Precautions for Electric Throttle Control System Calibration
servinfosub.ptxt: After performing any one of the following services, it is necessary to calibrate the completely closed and opened throttle valve positions stored in ECM.

item.ptxt: Replacement of ECM
Clearance of DTC related to electric throttle control system (P0607, P0122, P0123, P0222, P0223, P2100, P2101, P2119, P2122, P2123, P2127, P2128, P2135, P2138 or P2176)
Replacement of electric throttle body assembly or accelerator pedal assembly (incorporating APP sensor)
Disconnection of negative (–) cable at battery

servinfosub.title: Precautions for Generator Control System
servinfosub.ptxt: In connection with generating control of the generator, the engine speed may rise or drop due to such causal factors as described below.

item.ptxt: Battery is undercharged or its service life is used up.
The power for later added accessories is taken directly from the battery terminal.
A large amount of electric load is applied.
There is an abnormality with the generator control signal, generator field coil measured signal or in the current sensor system by following DTC.
P0515: Battery Temperature Sensor Circuit
P0620: Generator Control Circuit
P0622: Generator Field/F Terminal Circuit
P0A5A: Generator Current Sensor Circuit Range / Performance
